top_signals.title: European Gas Storage Can’t Survive 3 More Months of Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Europe could face a critical shortfall in natural gas stocks if shipping disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz persist for another 1-3 months, senior executives at Norwegian energy giant, Equinor ASA (NYSE:EQNR), have warned. Europe entered the current summer refill season with severely depleted gas reserves, with gas stores only 28% full following a prolonged winter. Europe’s storage levels are currently at 35-37%, significantly below the 50% seasonal norm, increasing the risk that the continent will miss its usual 90% target at the beginning…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Gold Gains as Prospects of Iran Deal Temper Inflation Concerns
top_signals.summary: Gold rose as signs that the US and Iran were closing in on a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz tempered inflation concerns.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
